page: Microsoft Azure DS13-2_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the DSv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 dedicated physical cores, providing 2 vCPUs with 1.0 thread per core. It is equipped with 56.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 28.0 GB per core. Additionally, the server includes 115 GB of local storage, while containing no GPU accelerators.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the processing capabilities of this instance are generally constrained. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests yielded scores of 1356.5 and 2688.07 respectively, both falling into the bottom 25% of tested hardware. Similarly, the Geekbench single-core score of 1053.0 and multi-core score of 1916.0 reflect lower-tier compute performance. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 177.59 GB/sec, but uncached large block read and write speeds drop to 19.35 GB/sec and 14.69 GB/sec respectively. Despite these constraints, the instance demonstrates average performance in LLM text generation using a small 135M model, achieving 90.73 tokens/sec.

The primary tradeoff of the DS13-2_v2 lies in its high memory capacity relative to its limited processing power. With 56.0 GB of RAM paired with only 2 vCPUs, the instance offers high resource density for memory-bound tasks that do not require intensive parallel computation. This makes it qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring large memory footprints but minimal CPU overhead, such as caching layers, lightweight database operations, or memory-intensive data processing. However, it is poorly suited for compute-heavy tasks like software compilation, ray tracing, or high-throughput web serving, where its benchmark scores fall in the bottom 10%.
description: Microsoft Azure DS13-2_v2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine instance within the DSv2 family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is configured with an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz, providing 2 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores. The instance is characterized by a high memory allocation of 56.0 GB (28.0 GB per core) and includes 115 GB of local storage, with no GPU capabilities. Benchmark data reveals constrained compute performance, with stress-ng and Geekbench scores placing in the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively. However, it maintains average performance in small-model LLM text generation at 90.73 tokens/sec. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads such as caching, database operations, and lightweight memory-bound data processing, offering a high memory-to-CPU ratio at the expense of raw processing throughput.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DS13-2_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs, 56.0 GB of RAM, and 115 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive workloads with low compute requirements.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-CPU ratio and local storage for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads.
